---
author: ""
header-includes:
   - \usepackage{rotating}
   - \usepackage{float}
   - \usepackage{natbib}
   - \usepackage{booktabs}
   - \usepackage{multirow}
geometry: "left=1cm,right=1cm,top=0.9cm,bottom=1.2cm"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    citation_package: natbib
    keep_tex: true
    number_sections: true
#bibliography: ref.bib
link-citations: yes
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
options(xtable.comment = FALSE)
library(reshape)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(naniar)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)
library(xtable)
library(this.path)
library(fs)

# set working directory to be the root of the code
setwd(this.path::this.dir())


for (stock_base in c("all", "large")) {
  # get correlations
  data <- readRDS(paste0("../../tmp/sym/", stock_base, "/summaries/cor_with_fm.RDS"))

  # rank the variables
  tmp <- readRDS(paste0("../../tmp/processed_signals/", stock_base, "/signal_availability.RDS")) %>%
    group_by(var, var_lab) %>%
    summarize(first_ym = min(yyyymm)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(first_ym) %>%
    mutate(var = gsub("_ret", "", var)) %>%
    bind_rows(., data.table(
      var = c("combined", "combined_1926_1962", "combined_1963_2023"),
      var_lab = c("Combined (full sample)", "Combined (1926-1962)", "Combined (1963-2023)"), first_ym = 1:3
    )) %>%
    arrange(first_ym) %>%
    mutate(idx = row_number()) %>%
    select(-first_ym) %>%
    mutate(var = ifelse(var == "indu", "industry",
      ifelse(var == "tec", "tech", var)
    )) %>%
    setDT()

  data <- merge(data, tmp, by = "var")
  rm(tmp)

  # get sample sizes
  sample_size <- data[, .(var_lab, obs)] %>%
    unique() %>%
    mutate(obs = comma(obs))

  # put into table format
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(cor_with_fm = sprintf("%.1f", 100 * cor_with_fm)) %>%
    mutate(cor_with_fm = paste0("$", cor_with_fm, "\\%$")) %>%
    dcast(idx + var_lab ~ var_type, value.var = "cor_with_fm") %>%
    inner_join(sample_size, by = c("var_lab")) %>%
    arrange(idx) %>%
    select(-idx) %>%
    setDT() %>%
    setcolorder(c("var_lab", "obs", "total", "sym", "asy"))
  rm(sample_size)

  # add col columns
  col_names <- data.table(t(as.data.frame(c("", paste0("(", 1:(ncol(data) - 1), ")")))))
  names(col_names) <- names(data)
  data <- rbind(col_names, data)
  rm(col_names)

  align_vector <- paste0("ll", paste0(rep("r", times = ncol(data) - 1), collapse = ""))
  x.side <- xtable(data, align = align_vector)

  this_add_to_row <- list(
    pos = list(-1, -1, -1, 3, nrow(data)),
    command = c(
      "\\toprule \\multirow{2}{*}{Predictor} & \\multirow{2}{*}{Obs} & \\multicolumn{3}{c}{Correlation with FM} \\\\\n \\cmidrule(l){3-5}",
      "& & CSM & Symmetric & Asymmetric \\\\\n \\cmidrule(l){2-5}",
      rep("\\vspace{5pt}", 2), "\\bottomrule"
    )
  )
  cat("The stock base is:", stock_base)
  print(x.side,
    type = "latex", table.placement = "!ht", include.rownames = FALSE,
    include.colnames = F,
    caption.placement = "top", # "top", NULL
    hline.after = NULL, # We don't need hline; we use booktabs
    floating = F, # whether \begin{Table} should be created (TRUE) or not (FALSE)
    sanitize.text.function = force, # Important to treat content of first column as latex function
    add.to.row = this_add_to_row
  )

  out_dir <- paste0(stock_base, "/")
  dir_create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  print(x.side,
    type = "latex", table.placement = "!ht", include.rownames = FALSE,
    include.colnames = F,
    caption.placement = "top", # "top", NULL
    hline.after = NULL, # We don't need hline; we use booktabs
    floating = F, # whether \begin{Table} should be created (TRUE) or not (FALSE)
    sanitize.text.function = force, # Important to treat content of first column as latex function
    add.to.row = this_add_to_row,
    file = paste0(stock_base, "/cor_with_fm.tex")
  )

  # # Define the path for the tex file
  # tex_file <- paste0(stock_base, "/cor_with_fm.tex")

  # # Create a standalone PDF from the tex snippet
  # # This wraps the table in a basic LaTeX document and compiles it
  # tinytex::latexmk(
  #   file = tex_file,
  #   engine = "pdflatex",
  #   install_packages = FALSE,
  #   emulate_gui = FALSE,
  #   # This 'template' wrapper is necessary because .tex from xtable is just a fragment
  #   args = c("-jobname=cor_with_fm", "-output-directory", out_dir)
  # )
}
```
